pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 51-52 searches for job name 'Experienced Developer'; Odoo 17 demo data (hr_demo.xml) confirms this is a standard job position
export_result.sh line 73 checks for 'Contract Signed'; verifier.py line 53 verifies stage matches 'Contract Signed'; Odoo 17 recruitment default stages confirm 'Contract Signed' as final hiring stage

setup_task.sh lines 68-76: applicant created without department_id field; verifier.py does not check department field; while 'Research & Development' exists in Odoo demo data, it is not assigned to Sofia Martinez applicant

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic applicant named Sofia Martinez for the 'Experienced Developer' job position in Odoo's Recruitment app. The metadata claims the applicant is in 'Research & Development' department, but the applicant is created without explicit department assignment in the code. Verified facts: applicant_name is 'Sofia Martinez', job_position is 'Experienced Developer' (a standard Odoo demo job position), and target_stage is 'Contract Signed' (a standard Odoo recruitment stage). Task requires advancing the applicant to 'Contract Signed' stage and creating an employee record linked to the applicant.
